title: Official Information Act (OIA) requests | Stats NZ
content:
## Official Information Act (OIA) requests
You can request government information (official information) and expect it to be made available unless there is a good reason to withhold it.
The Official Information Act 1982 (or OIA) allows New Zealand citizens (as well as permanent residents, visitors to New Zealand, and body corporates with a place of business in New Zealand) the right to request official information. This promotes openness and transparency, enables greater public participation in government, and holds the Government and government agencies to account.
The OIA for Ministers and agencies on the Ombudsman New Zealand website has more information about established guidelines on how agencies manage OIA requests.
- Before you submit an OIA
- Making an OIA request
- Response times and charges
- Following up on your response or making a complaint
- Our published responses to requests

## Before you submit an OIA
The information you are looking for may already be publicly available on our website. Have a look at these resources:
- Our publications – including information releases, surveys, and collections.
- Our corporate publications – including annual reports, strategic intentions, briefings to incoming Ministers, Cabinet papers.
- Our published responses to OIA requests – all previous responses to OIA requests since 2017. These responses are proactively released monthly.

### For data or statistical information
- Our table-building tools (such as NZ.Stat and Infoshare where you can view and download large datasets)
- Our methods, standards, and research page
- Customised data services has more information about this service and the types of data we can provide.

### For personal information that Stats NZ holds about you
Requests for personal information are managed in accordance with the Privacy Act 2020. If you want to make a privacy request, you can contact us by:
- Phone: 0508 525 525 (toll-free)
- Email: info@stats.govt.nz

## Making an OIA request
Your request should be as clear and specific as you can possibly make it.
Please provide your name, contact address (email or postal), and details of the information you want.
officeoftheGSCE@stats.govt.nz
Stats NZ PO Box 2922 Wellington 6140
0508 525 525 (toll-free)

## Response times and charges
We will acknowledge your request once we receive it and a decision will be communicated to you as soon as possible, and no later than 20 working days after we receive your request.
For large requests, or those that require consultation, the OIA allows for an extension on the time limit. If more time is needed to make a decision on your request, we will let you know and give you a specific due date.
You can see when you may expect a response by using the Office of the Ombudsman Response Calculator .
The State Services Commission publishes data on the number of requests we receive each year and the timeliness of our responses: Official Information Act statistics .
Requesting official information is free, though we may consider charging for the supply of the information requested if a considerable amount of work is involved to make it available. We use the charging guidelines for OIA requests set out by the Ministry of Justice and will contact you to discuss this.

## Following up on your response or making a complaint
If you have any questions, or are dissatisfied with our response, feel free to contact us in the first instance as we may be able to quickly resolve the issue.
If you email us at officeoftheGSCE@stats.govt.nz , we will respond to you in a timely manner.
You can make a complaint to the Office of the Ombudsman if you have concerns regarding the decision we made on your request; or if you are unhappy about the way your request was treated or processed.
These concerns can relate to:
- the withholding of information
- extending the timeframe to respond to you
- any charges for providing the information you have requested
- delays in providing you with a decision or the information
- your request being transferred.
The Office of the Ombudsman can investigate and review our decision and may make a recommendation to us if it is considered appropriate.
Information about how to make a complaint is available at www.ombudsman.parliament.nz or 0800 802 602 .

## Our published responses to requests
Responses to Official Information Act requests has the requests we’ve responded to since July 2017. These responses are proactively released monthly with any personal details removed.
Page updated 8 December 2023
